page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, though it does not include local NVMe SSD storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8gb.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. It achieves top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth tests, both for read and write operations, as well as in Passmark memory and database operations. Multi-threaded performance is highly competitive, scoring in the top tier for Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the average category. This indicates a design optimized for parallelized, memory-intensive workloads rather than raw single-threaded clock speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based ARMv9 architecture delivers high resource density and memory throughput per unit of compute, offering a favorable qualitative profile for scale-out deployments. The combination of 384.0 GB of RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong multi-core capabilities makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications. Recommended workloads include large-scale database operations, Redis in-memory caching, static web serving, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the r8gb.12xlarge delivers top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing. Conversely, single-core compute performance remains average. This hardware profile offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound, highly parallelized applications. It is highly suited for database hosting, Redis caching, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server featuring 48 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized platform delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
